name: From Talhadas to São João do Monte — circular route
name_original: De Talhadas a São João do Monte — percurso circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Talhadas traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and rural roads, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often including substantial climbs and descents. Cyclists can expect to encounter a mix of open countryside and forested sections, providing diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Talhadas The most popular road cycling route is From Talhadas to São João do Monte — circular route, a 44.2 miles (71.1 km) difficult trail that takes about 4 hours to complete, featuring significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Paradela Railway Station – Oliveira de Frades loop from Estação Ferroviária de Paradela (desactivada), a difficult 40.3 miles (64.8 km) path that winds through rural landscapes with notable climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Ponte do Poço de S. Tiago – Striped Houses of Costa Nova loop from Estação Ferroviária de Paradela (desactivada), a 79.0 miles (127.1 km) trail leading through varied scenery, often completed in about 5 hours 28 minutes. Road cycling around Talhadas is defined by hilly terrain, rural roads, and open countryside. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Talhadas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Talhadas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Talhadas?
A: There are over 30 road cycling routes around Talhadas, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: Are there road cycling routes for all skill levels in Talhadas?
A: Yes, the road cycling network around Talhadas caters to various abilities. You'll find options ranging from easy routes, with 2 available, to moderate (18 routes) and difficult (14 routes) paths, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Talhadas?
A: Road cycling routes around Talhadas are characterized by rolling hills and rural roads. Cyclists should expect significant elevation changes, including substantial climbs and descents, as well as a mix of open countryside and forested sections.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Talhadas?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular. For example, the popular route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384054104/from-talhadas-to-sao-joao-do-monte-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Talhadas to São João do Monte — circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 44.2-mile loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32153879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela Station – Talhadas Parish Church loop from Talhadas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 31.4-mile ride.

Q: What are some of the longer road cycling routes in the Talhadas area?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19825748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte do Poço de S. Tiago – Striped Houses of Costa Nova loop from Estação Ferroviária de Paradela (desactivada)&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 79.0-mile trail. Another challenging option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384054104/from-talhadas-to-sao-joao-do-monte-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Talhadas to São João do Monte — circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 44.2 miles.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes pass by notable natural and historical points. You might encounter the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527671" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabreia Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3864876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poço de S. Tiago Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region's varied landscape offers many picturesque spots.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Talhadas?
A: The komoot community highly rates the road cycling experience in Talhadas, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which includes rolling hills and rural roads, and the diverse riding experiences through open countryside and forested sections.

Q: Are there any disused railway lines converted into cycle paths near Talhadas?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4979779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ecopista do Vouga: Sernada do Vouga to Paradela&lt;/a&gt; is a notable example. This converted railway line offers a different cycling experience, often flatter and more accessible than the hilly road routes.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or facilities I might see near the routes?
A: Along your rides, you might pass by points of interest such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3864826" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela Station&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5918595" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedrim Station&lt;/a&gt;, both offering glimpses into the region's past infrastructure.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty road cycling routes in Talhadas?
A: Yes, there are 18 moderate difficulty routes available. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32153879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradela Station – Talhadas Parish Church loop from Talhadas&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.4-mile route with notable elevation changes, suitable for intermediate cyclists.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Talhadas?
A: Given the region's rolling hills and varied terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Talhadas, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring cooler, wetter weather.
tips: Next to the Alfusqueiro River Park, on a bend in the river, is this beautiful bridge. There is evidence that the original dates back to Roman times and was rebuilt in the 18th century. In addition to being a bridge, it is a charming viewpoint of the park and the river.

The Alfusqueiro River Park, located near Águeda, offers an idyllic river beach on the Alfusqueiro River, with natural grass and a large sandy beach. It has a shaded picnic area, bar and views of Serra do Caramulo. It is a peaceful place and perfect for families, with calm, clear waters and modern infrastructure, including parking, toilets and picnic areas.

São João do Monte River Beach, also known as Paraíso River Beach, is a calm and great place to spend the day by the river. It offers infrastructure such as a bar, snacks and bathrooms, as well as a historic Romanesque bridge.
The Cercosa Dam, on the Alfusqueiro river in Vouzela, district of Viseu, began operations in 1994. It is a gravity dam with a hydroelectric plant, capable of producing 9.65 Mio. kWh annually.

The main church, originally in Doninhas, moved to its current location between 1686 and 1707. It has baroque gilded altars and a ceiling decorated with paintings of the Passion of Christ and saints. In the churchyard, ancient olive trees and the rectory stand out. Every year, the Parade of the Three Kings takes place, a tradition that has been going on for over 50 years.

waypoints.name: São João do Monte River Beach and Romanesque Bridge
